answer: Cynewulf wrote three works that are in the Falsafa corpus: (1) Andreas (cynewulf-andreas-07b573), an Old English poem recounting the life and missionary journey of Saint Andrew among the cannibalistic Mermedonians; (2) Elene (cynewulf-elene-d2d132), an Old English poem narrating the finding of the True Cross by Saint Helena, mother of Constantine the Great; and (3) Juliana (cynewulf-juliana-9a2157), an Old English hagiographic poem telling the story of Saint Juliana of Nicomedia. All three works are from the Medieval era and classified as Advanced difficulty Old English literature.
